title: Contains Duplicates
steps: Create an empty set/hashmap to store seen numbers
Loop the entire list of numbers
If the number exists in the hashmap, return True
Otherwise, add it to the hashmap

title: Valid Anagram
steps: Create empty count hashes for both strings
Check if the length are not the same, do an early return
Loop through the index of one string
Check if that character in i position of both strings and add the count to the hash
Finally return if the hash of both strings are equal

title: Two Sum
steps: Create an empty hash for the numbers storing their value: index
Create a potential variable which subtracts the num at current position by the target
If the potential is in the hash, return the array with the current index and the value (which stores the index) of the hash
Otherwise, add the current number to the hash as the key, and the index as the value

title: Group Anagrams
steps: Create an empty hash to store the sorted string as the key and the list of anagrams as the value
Loop through the input array of strings, create a count array of size 26 to count the frequency of each character in the string
Loop through the characters of the string to find the index, and add to the count array
Convert the count array to a tuple and use it as a key in the hash, and append the original string to the list of anagrams for that key
Finally, return all the values of the hash as a list
time_complexity: O(n * k) where n is the number of strings and k is the maximum length of a string, due to iterating through each string and its characters
space_complexity: O(n * k) where n is the number of strings and k is the maximum length of a string, due to storing all strings in the hash
